provisions.title: Citation and commencement
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Tanzania National Parks Staff Regulations, 2011.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These Regulations shall apply to employees of the Tanzania National Parks.

provisions.title: Definitions
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context requires otherwise— “ Act ” means the National Parks Act as amended from time to time; “ an increment stopped ” means non-payment for a specified period of an increment otherwise due; “ appointing authority ” means the Board of Trustees or a body or organ or person empowered by the Board to make appointments and promotions; “ appointment ” means engagement in the service of the organization or promotion to a higher scale or grade; “ Appointment and Disciplinary Committee ” in its acronym (ADC) when used in relation to the Board , means the Appointments and Disciplinary Committee of the Board and when used in relation to the management , means the Appointments and Disciplinary Committee of the Management; “ Board ” means the Board of Trustees of the Tanzania National Parks as established and provided for under the Act ; “ Chairman ” when used in relation to the Board of Trustees , means the Chairman of the Board ; " contractual employee " means an employee whose engagement is not on permanent and pensionable terms but on special terms, conditions and for a specific period of time; “ child ” means an employee ’s child, step child or legally adopted child under the age of eighteen; “ domicile ” means a place within Tanzania where an employee has, at the time of his appointment , or thereafter declared in his Personal Particulars Form to be his home; “ date of first appointment ” means a date when an employee first assumes duty with the organization ; “ disciplinary authority ” means the Board of Trustees or a body or organ or person empowered by the Board to take disciplinary measures; “ employee ” means an individual who has entered into a contract of employment with the Tanzania National Parks but shall not include Tanzania National Parks’ clients or customers of any profession, business or undertaking carried on by the individual or contracted to perform a specific task; “ employer ” means the Tanzania National Parks; “ family ” means the employee ’s spouse and not more than four children of the couple whose age does not exceed 18 years and also includes father and mother of an employee of the age of or above 60 years who are solely dependent on him; “ management ” when used in relation to the organization , means the organization ’s Chief Executive Officer together with his directorate and departmental heads authorized to act on behalf of the Board in the day to day operations of the organization ; when used in relation to the Parks, means the Park Manager together with departmental heads authorized to act on behalf of the Trustees ; “ Minister ” means Minister of the Ministry responsible for National Parks; “ OPRAS ” means Open Performance Review and Appraisal System; “ organization ” means the Tanzania National Parks; “ part-time employee ” means a person appointed to work on a regular basis but on temporary terms and whose number of hours of work each week is less than full working hours in a week; “ secretary ” means secretary to the Board of Trustees appointed under paragraph 8 to the Second Schedule of the National Parks Act “ staff ” means persons in the service of the Tanzania National Parks; “ Trustees ” means the Board of Trustees established under section 8 . Part II – Appointments of staff

provisions.title: Authority for appointments
provisions.content: Appointment of all staff of the organization shall be made by the Trustees ; however, the Trustees may, delegate their powers of appointment in respect of any category of employees to anybody or organ or person within the organization .

provisions.title: Categories of appointments
provisions.content: Appointments in the service of the organization shall be of the following categories— (a) unspecified period of time; (b) contractual; and (c) contract for a specific task which does not exceed sixty days.

provisions.title: Conditions of appointments
provisions.content: Unless it is otherwise provided by law, every employee holds his or her appointment subject to— (a) these Regulations and to any directives, rulings or circulars issued by the Trustees ; and (b) the terms of his letter of appointment and, in the case of a contractual, part-time or casual employee , to the terms of his or letter of engagement.

provisions.title: Delegation
provisions.content: (1) The Trustees may delegate, either generally or in any specific case, any power exercisable by or duty imposed on them under these Regulations. (2) The Trustees may from time to time amend or repeal any of these Regulations and may, in specific cases, waive any of the provisions of these Regulations.

provisions.title: Staffing policy
provisions.content: (1) The employee of the organization shall be citizens of the United Republic of Tanzania; however, non-citizen staff may be employed on short contractual terms in cases where it is proven that Tanzanian citizens of requisite qualification and experience is not found. (2) Subject to the provisions of regulation 5 , no person who is beyond the age of fifty years shall be appointed on unspecified period of time.

provisions.title: Interviews
provisions.content: The opportunities for employment in the organization shall be advertised in the print, electronic media or public places, stating the qualifications required, job description, conditions and terms of service and shall be subjected to interview.

provisions.title: Selection of candidates
provisions.content: (1) The selection of candidates in different posts in the organization shall be based on merits through an open competition by conducting interviews. (2) In order to ensure that selection of candidates for interview is based on merit through open competition, internally and externally, the Trustees shall appoint an adhoc committee to shortlist the candidates. (3) The Trustees shall prepare and issue guidelines to be followed in conducting interviews, and shall conduct interviews for appointments in the organization , so as to ensure objectivity, consistency and to attain the right qualities of the applicants. (4) Candidates with the same degree of preference, qualifications and experience, proven merit and suitability for the posts in question shall be accorded greater weight than seniority: Provided that where the respective candidates are men and women priority shall be given to women. (5) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-regulation (4) , selected candidate shall, before being appointed in the organization, undergo medical examination to prove his medical fitness or otherwise.

provisions.title: Medical examination
provisions.content: (1) Every successful applicant for unspecified period of time or contractual employment in the organization shall undergo medical examination and— (a) such medical examination shall determine the applicant’s fitness and shall be conducted by a Medical Officer; (b) the requests for medical examination and certification of an applicant’s fitness shall be made on the form prescribed in Appendix TNP/F. l to these Regulations. (2) No candidate shall be rejected merely on the basis of HIV/A1DS status or physical disability, provided that the applicant has required physical strength or qualities.

provisions.title: Letters of appointment
provisions.content: (1) Every person shall, on appointment , be given either a letter of appointment or a contract setting out the terms of his appointment and specifying the date on which the appointment shall commence. (2) Letters of Appointment to successful applicants shall be in the form prescribed in Appendices TNP/F.2 to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Personal Record
provisions.content: (1) Newly appointed employees shall be required to complete a Staff Particulars Record Form as prescribed in Appendix TNP/F.3 to these Regulations, which shall be updated from time to time by the employee as approved in writing by the employer and maintained by the organization . (2) The contents of Staff Particulars Record Form shall include— (a) name, age, permanent address and sex of the employee ; (b) place of recruitment; (c) job description; (d) date of commencement; (e) type of the contract; (f) place of work; (g) hours of work; (h) remuneration, the method for its calculation and details of any benefits or payment in kind; and (i) any other prescribed matter. (3) The employer shall keep the written particulars prescribed in sub-regulation (2) for a period of five years after the termination of employment. (4) An employee may change his status (eg. Name, religion, place of domicile , marital) upon submitting to the Director General relevant certificates or legal documents from relevant authorities.

provisions.title: Probationary period
provisions.content: (1) Every person appointed to any post, shall be on probation for a minimum period of twelve months, provided that the probation period may, if deemed necessary, be extended by an additional period not exceeding one year. (2) Where an employee is promoted to any post in the organization he shall be on probation for a period of six months and if deemed necessary, be extended by an additional period not exceeding six months. (3) Where an employee fails to satisfactorily perform the duties of the post to which he was recruited or promoted, the sanction procedures applicable in the OPRAS shall apply.

provisions.title: Performance reports of employees on probation
provisions.content: (1) The Trustees may direct the Director General to furnish performance reports on the conduct and suitability of selected employees on probation upon completion of the twelve months service and may require such reports on other employees on probation to be forwarded to the Director General by respective heads of directorate, department and Park Managers. (2) The performance reports referred to under subregulation (1) shall clearly address themselves to the conduct and capability of the employees and recommendation for confirmation or otherwise. (3) Any such report shall be discussed and agreed upon between the appraiser and the appraisee but the substance of any adverse performance report shall be communicated to the employee in writing by the Director General.

provisions.title: Confirmation of appointment of employees on probation
provisions.content: At the end of the period of probation, the employee may be confirmed in his appointment and given a letter to this effect unless his probationary period is extended or his services are terminated on unsatisfactory performance.

provisions.title: Appointment on contract
provisions.content: (1) Where a senior employee is appointed on contract, the contract may be entered into on behalf of the Trustees by the Chairman or a Trustee appointed by the Trustees for that purpose and by the Secretary. (2) The conditions of service of an employee appointed on contract shall be in accordance with regulation 6 of these Regulations.

provisions.title: Acting appointment
provisions.content: The Chairman shall, upon consultation with other Trustees , endorse the name of any senior staff proposed to act for the Director General in the event the Director General is unable for any reason to discharge the functions.

provisions.title: Promotions
provisions.content: A contractual employees shall be subject to these Regulations, directives or circulars of the Board of Trustees and save in so far as it is otherwise specified in these Regulations or in his letter of appointment .

provisions.title: Contractual appointments
provisions.content: (1) Any promotion of an employee shall be based on staff establishment, scheme of service, seniority, merit, effective and efficient performance, qualification, skills and personal qualities. (2) Where the Trustees have promoted an employee , a letter of promotion in the form set out in Appendix TNP/F.4 to these Regulations, shall be issued to the employee .

provisions.title: Employee to submit himself to Medical examination
provisions.content: The Director General shall, after consultation with the ailing employee , request the Chief Medical Officer to the Ministry responsible for Health to appoint a Medical Board with a view to ascertaining whether the employee has become incapable by reason of any physical or medical infirmity of performing the duties of his office, provided that the Director General shall have no alternative work for such employee before he opts for the Medical Board .

provisions.title: Compliance with lawful orders
provisions.content: Every employee so ordered shall comply with all lawful orders given to him by his superiors.

provisions.title: Posting
provisions.content: An employee shall, at the time of recruitment, and in writing be required to serve at such places in Tanzania as the Trustees or Director General may direct.

provisions.title: Conduct of employees
provisions.content: Every employee of Tanzania National Parks shall adhere to the Codes of Ethics as set out in the First Schedule to these Regulations and such an employee shall not conduct himself at any time in a manner as to bring the Trustees into disrepute.

provisions.title: Absence without permission
provisions.content: (1) An employee shall not be absent from duty without permission, otherwise absence of each day from duty shall be regarded as a breach of the employment contract under these Regulations. (2) Where an employee has committed a breach under sub-regulation (1) of these Regulations, the Director General, Head of Directorate, Department or Park Manager, shall immediately take all necessary steps to inform the employee to that effect and if the employee fails to respond or to offer satisfactory explanation as to his absence, the employee shall be liable to disciplinary action and be punished pursuant to these Regulations. (3) Without prejudice to the provision of sub-regulation (2) , disciplinary proceedings in respect of an employee absent from duty without leave or reasonable cause for a period exceeding five days and no reply to a charge of absence from duty without leave as received from him within ten days of the dispatch of the charge to him, he shall be liable to termination. (4) An employee travelling outside Tanzania during his leave shall notify the employer who shall, in turn, acknowledge such notification. (5) An employee who is absent from Tanzania while on leave without notification to the employer shall be liable to disciplinary action under these Regulations.

provisions.title: Publication by employees
provisions.content: No employee shall divulge any information relating to the business of the employer in any form, without the express permission of the employer .

provisions.title: Pecuniary embarrassment
provisions.content: Serious pecuniary embarrassment from whatever cause shall be regarded as necessarily impairing the efficiency of an employee and rendering him liable to disciplinary proceedings.

provisions.title: Annual opras report
provisions.content: (1) The organization shall undertake Open Performance Review and Appraisal System ( OPRAS ) for all its employees. (2) For the purpose of this regulation, the performance appraisal shall be discussed with the employee in order to discover, evaluate and document the potential or shortcomings of the individual so as to allow measures to be taken for improvement of the efficiency and effectiveness of the staff. (3) The Management shall ensure that the assessment criteria and those responsible for conducting OPRAS are acting fairly, with no discrimination. (4) The information obtained through OPRAS shall be used in awarding or withholding annual salary increments, planning job rotation, training programmes and in making appointments to higher posts, promotion, demotion or termination of the appointment to the particular post. (5) Every employee shall be given a job description incorporating specific objectives and measurable indicators for results to be achieved. Such results shall on annual basis be reviewed. (6) An employee shall be given feedback at regular intervals of not less than six months on his performance against the objectives and shall be given advice and support to improve any shortcomings. (7) Subject to these Regulations, where the performance of an employee is adjudged unsatisfactory and there is a need for demotion, termination of employment or to withhold annual salary increment, the employer or an authorized employee shall notify the concerned employee in writing indicating the area or areas in which his performance has deficient and giving him an opportunity to improve on the same within a period to be determined by the employer or such authorized employee . (8) Where after the expiry of the period specified under sub regulation (7) the employee’s performance does not show improvement, the employer or authorized employee shall submit recommendation to the relevant disciplinary authority for either demote, terminate services or withhold the annual salary increment for such employee. (9) Where there are disagreements on the performance appraisal process, the supervisor shall advise the appraisee to report the matter to the immediate superior of the supervisor in writing. (10) Every performance appraisal report shall be duly filled and the supervisor shall ensure that one copy remains in the employee ’s personal file and another copy is retained by the appraisee.

provisions.title: Termination of appointment
provisions.content: (1) The termination of appointment shall be effected in accordance with the terms and conditions of service incorporated in the contract or a letter of appointment . (2) The termination of any appointment including probationary may be effected by giving one month notice in writing on either side or one month’s gross salary in lieu thereof; such notice may include earned leave. (3) Any employee on contract or agreement terms may resign his appointment in accordance with the relevant provisions of his contract/agreement or letter of appointment and where no such specific provision has been made; sub-regulation (2) shall apply. (4) Notice for termination shall not be given— (a) during any period of leave taken under these Regulations; or (b) to run concurrently with any such period of leave.

provisions.title: Grounds for termination of service
provisions.content: The services of an employee shall be terminated on the following grounds— (a) on attaining the compulsory retirement age; (b) on medical grounds that is where an employee has by reasons of any disease of body or mind, become unable to discharge his duties efficiently on recommendations of a Medical Board ; (c) where the post held by such an employee and the services for which he was employed have been abolished and the employee has therefore become redundant; (d) where the appointing authority finds that the interests of the organization are in jeopardy due to general and sustained employee ’s inefficiency which cannot be attributed to negligence or failing health; (e) where termination is a result of a disciplinary action awarded; and (f) failure to renew a fixed term contract on the same or similar terms if there was a reasonable expectation of renewal of the contract.

provisions.title: Refund of advances, debts upon resignation or termination
provisions.content: An employee who resigns or is terminated from service shall be required to refund any outstanding advances and debts which have been granted to him by the organization .

provisions.title: Certificate of service
provisions.content: (1) The organization shall on termination, issue an employee with a certificate of service. (2) The Certificate issued shall state the positions held by the employee , period of service, the reason for the termination of employment, and brief remarks on general conduct and efficiency and such a certificate shall be in the Form presented in appendix TNP/F.6 to these Regulations. (3) The details for assessment of efficiency and conduct shall be in the following format— (a) “Very Good”; (b) “Good”; (c) “Fail;”; or (d) “Poor”.

provisions.title: Retirement age
provisions.content: (1) An employee may retire voluntarily from the service upon attaining the age of fifty five years and compulsorily after attaining the age of sixty years as provided for by the Public Service Retirement Benefits Act. (2) An employee who attains the age of voluntary or compulsory retirement shall give a notice of his impending retirement in writing to his appointing authority at least six months before the date of cessation of duty. (3) Where the year and not the actual date of birth of an employee has been recorded, his age shall be determined by deeming his date of birth to be the first day of July of the year. (4) Where only the month of birth has been recorded, the date of birth shall be deemed to be the sixteenth day of that month or fifteenth in the case of February. (5) Where there is ambiguity on the date of birth of an employee and where there is argument between the employer and the employee as to the accurate date of birth, the date on record disclosed by the employee during his first employment, shall be recognized as the accurate date of birth of such an employee . (6) An employee whose employment is terminated for whatever reason is obliged to return to the employer his identity card and any other employer ’s property which is under his custody.

provisions.title: Staff Pension scheme
provisions.content: The employee shall make sure that every employee has joined a Pension Scheme. Part III – Salaries, increments and allowances emoluments

provisions.title: Deductions
provisions.content: (1) Staff salary scales shall be those prescribed by the Trustees in the organization ’s approved Scheme of Service. (2) The commencing salary of an employee on first appointment shall be the minimum within the relevant scale. (3) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub regulation (2) , an employee on first appointment may be appointed at a commencing salary above the minimum of the approved scale if his qualifications, experience and existing salary warrant such situation. (4) Salary shall be paid monthly in arrears except where the Trustees direct otherwise. (5) When under these Regulations an employee is liable to refund or pay any amount of money to the organization , such amount shall be deducted from the salary or any other sums due to the employee . (6) The employer may deduct from the employee ’s salary where the deduction is— (a) permitted under a written law; (b) due to wage determination; (c) in implementing a court order; (d) in implementing an arbitration award; (e) agreed in writing by the employee ; and (f) an implementation of a collective agreement. (7) The total deductions from the employee ’s salary under sub regulation (6) shall not exceed one quarter of the employee’s salary. (8) An employee on contract for a specific period of time or task shall not have automatic right on any change of employees’ salary or other benefits as approved by the Trustees from time to time.

provisions.title: When increment is granted and stopped
provisions.content: (1) Where for any reason it is decided to stop an increment, the employee shall be advised in writing of the decision stating the period for which the increment is stopped provided that an employee ’s increment shall not be stopped without such an employee having first been given an opportunity to show cause why his increment should not be stopped. (2) All employees employed or promoted between l st January and 30 th June, will receive their increments on 1 st January of every subsequent year and those employed or promoted between 1 st July and 31 st December will receive their increments on 1 st July of every subsequent year— (a) although an employee in an incremental scale shall not be entitled to any increment as a right, he shall normally be granted such increment as a reward for good past performance; (b) unless otherwise provided at the time of his engagement, service for increments shall be reckoned from the date of taking up an appointment save that in the case of new appointee from overseas the date shall be that of arrival in Tanzania. (3) An increment is granted with effect from the first day of the month in which it falls due. (4) When an increment is not granted at all during an incremental year, for whatever reason normally only one increment shall be granted next incremental date; however, in an exceptional case and with the sanction of the disciplinary authority an employee may be granted additional increment, or such increment as may bring his salary to that point in the scale which he may have reached had his increment been granted when due. (5) Appointing authority may award additional increment or increments to any employee who performs his duties outstandingly but docs not secure promotion due to unavoidable factors such as lack of vacancies: Provided that the Appointing Authority shall not award more than two increments at any one time. (6) There shall be a proficiency bar at the top of every salary scale and no employee shall be allowed to cross the bar into a higher salary scale except on promotion to a higher post or when the position he is filling has been upgraded by the Trustees .

provisions.title: Acting allowance
provisions.content: (1) An acting allowance shall be paid to an employee appointed to act in the office of the Director General, provided that— (a) prior thereto, authority of the Trustees for the acting appointment has been obtained; (b) he has acted in that office for a continuous period of not less than fourteen days and (c) in no case the allowance payable shall exceed one half of the employee ’s substantive salary. (2) Where an acting allowance is payable, the amount thereof shall be the difference between the employee ’s substantive salary and the minimum salary of the office in which he is appointed to act. (3) Where an acting allowance is payable it shall be paid with effect from the date the employee concerned is appointed in an acting capacity. (4) Acting appointments with acting allowance— (a) in other duty positions of the organization , shall be made in writing by the Director General at the time of approving the absence of the position holder and be reported to the Board for approval. (b) the period of acting allowance shall commence after thirty days of absence of post holder. (c) it shall be the duty of the head of the relevant directorate to report immediately to the Director General any vacant duty position for appointment of an acting officer.

provisions.title: Entertainment allowance
provisions.content: (1) The Trustees may authorize a modest budget for receipted entertainment which shall be spent at the discretion of the Director General or other authorized senior officers of the organization to afford them the ability to offer hospitality for establishing social contacts for the development and welfare of the organization . (2) The Trustees shall from time to time approve an unreceipted entertainment allowance for specified senior officers of the organization to afford them the ability to offer hospitality to those most directly concerned with their fields of responsibilities and to enable them to establish personal contacts in social environments. (3) The Trustees may authorize payment of entertainment allowances to senior officers of organizations, to afford them the ability to offer hospitality to those most directly concerned with their fields of responsibilities and also, generally, to enable them to establish personal contacts in social environments. (4) Where entertainment takes place in a hotel or restaurant, reimbursement shall be on the basis of actual expenditure supported by documentary evidence like hotel bills and invoices.

provisions.title: Subsistence allowance
provisions.content: (1) Where an employee is transferred from, one station to another, a disturbance allowance at the rate of ten per cent of the employee ’s annual substantive gross salary as may be reviewed from time to time by the Trustees shall be paid to him at the current station. (2) An employee who is transferred from one station to another on promotion, his annual substantive gross salary for the purpose of calculating his disturbance allowance shall be taken as being that of the post to which he has been promoted. (3) Movement within a Park shall not constitute a transfer since the whole Park is considered to be within an employee ’s “station”, hence no Disturbance Allowance shall be paid for such movement.

provisions.title: Incidental allowances when travelling outside Tanzania
provisions.content: (1) An employee who is away, from his station on duty shall be entitled to subsistence allowance a night out allowance in respect of each night for which he is absent at rates approved by the Trustees from time to time and— (a) an employee travelling on duty who does not spend a night away from his duty station but who is absent there from for six hours or more in anyone day shall be entitled for fifty percent (50%) per diem of that station; (b) an employee who travels on duty away from duty station for more than six hours shall be entitled for fifty percent (50%) of subsistence allowance as transit allowance which shall be determined from time to time by the Board of Trustees ; and (c) other expenses related to transportation between home or hotel on arrival or departure station, shall be reimbursed in addition to the subsistence allowance. (2) An employee on first appointment shall be entitled, at his duty station, to fourteen (14) days subsistence allowance and any nights spent on journey from his place of engagement to his duty station for self, spouse and up to four children. (3) An employee on transfer shall be entitled to subsistence allowance for self, spouse and up to four children upon arrival at his new station: Provided that no payment shall be made for more than fourteen days. (4) Eligibility for daily subsistence allowance on first appointment and transfer each referred to by sub-regulation (2) and (3) of this regulation shall be paid at full rate to the employee and spouse and at half rate to the children. (5) An employee who is transferred solely at his own request, to suit his own convenience, shall be eligible for the grant of transport entitlements, under the following circumstances— (a) where an employee transferred has been working in one station continuously for five years or more; (b) where an employee is transferred in preparation for retirement and he has been working away from his region for a continuous period of ten years or more; and (c) where an employee is transferred to join his spouse. (6) Notwithstanding the provision of sub regulation (5) , the Director General may, in his discretion, grant transport entitlements to a an employee whose transfer has been arranged at his own request: Provided that such transfer would not results in unnecessary expenditure to the organization as in a situation where the vacancy would have otherwise been filled by the appointment . (7) Where an employee attends fully paid trips such as seminars, workshops, short courses and conferences sponsored by any other organization or individual, he shall be paid twenty percent (20%) of the normally applicable subsistence allowance rates by the organization to cover incidental expenses: Provided that the trip does not exceed ninety days. (8) When the subsistence expenses of an employee travelling on duty outside Tanzania are met by the organization he shall be entitled to receive additional twenty (20) percent of the approved subsistence allowance to cover incidental expenses. (9) Upon completion of a long term training of more than two years an employee shall be eligible for subsistence allowance as set out in sub regulation (3) above when reporting back to his former or new work station.

provisions.title: On call allowances to medical staff
provisions.content: On call allowance at a rate to be determined by the Trustees shall be paid to the following medical staff who after a nightshift, cannot be granted a day off due to exigencies of service— (a) Assistant Medical Officer; (b) Clinical Officer; (c) Laboratory Technologist; and (d) Nurse.

provisions.title: Overtime and extra duty allowances
provisions.content: The general consideration shall govern the payment of overtime and extra duty allowanccs— (a) The remuneration of all employees is fixed on the assumption that their whole time is at the disposal of the organization . Payment for overtime or extra duty allowance can therefore only be justified by exceptional circumstances which cannot be met in any other way; and (b) Large number of posts in the service such as Park Rangers and accountants cannot be considered to have regular fixed working hours. By reasons of the nature of duties of these posts hours are of necessity long and irregular the holders therefore arc given time-off after an usually arduous spells of duty.

provisions.title: Circumstances under which overtime may be paid
provisions.content: The Director General may authorize the payment of overtime to all employees where the following conditions are fully satisfied— (a) The employee concerned has been in personal attendance upon the public beyond normal hours, or has been called upon to carry out work at some specific time outside normal working hours, which can be done at no other time; (b) The employee cannot be compensated by time off during normal working hours; and (c) The overtime worked has been supervised by an employee who is not himself eligible for overtime allowance.

provisions.title: Housing allowance
provisions.content: (1) The organization shall endeavor to provide accommodation to its employees. (2) Where an employee is not allocated an organization ’s house the employer shall pay him housing allowance commensurate to his rank: Provided that where married couples are employees of the organization who live together in one house, such allowance will be paid to only one employee .

provisions.title: Honorarium
provisions.content: An employee of the organization who makes special contributions to the organization which lead to greater efficiency or enhanced reputation of the organization may be eligible for the payment of an honorarium of an amount which the Director General considers reasonable and justified under the circumstances.

provisions.title: Circumstances under which free transport is granted
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provisions of these Regulations and any other relevant written laws, an employee shall be granted free transport under the following circumstances— (a) on first appointment ; (b) on termination of appointment ; (c) on retirement; and (d) on transfer; (e) on duty; (f) on leave; (g) on medical treatment; or (h) on attending seminars, conferences or in - service training approved by the employer . (2) On death of the employee , transport of the body shall be provided to the place of burial at the station at which the death occurred or to the place of domicile : Provided that in all circumstances stated in paragraphs (a) to (d) of sub-regulation (1) transport shall be provided to an employee’s place of domicile, in all circumstances stated in Sub-regulation (1) the employee shall be entitled to free transport for himself, spouse and children as well as his personal belongings not exceeding 5 tons for employees whose salary scales are from G.6 up to G.13 and 3tons for employees whose salary scales are from G. l to G.5 and TAN 01 to TAN 05. (3) The employer shall incur the costs of burial on the death of an employee , spouse, child , father or mother: Provided that the parent was staying with the employee at the time of death; (4) The costs of burial borne by the employer shall include— (a) coffin; (b) grave; (c) shroud; (d) food and beverages for two days particularly the day of death and that of burial at a place of funeral to be determined by the responsible relative(s) of the deceased; (e) wreath; and (f) transport from the point of death to the home place or any other place of burial as may be decided by the employee or his official representative as the case may be, whichever is nearer. (5) An employee who resigns from employment shall not be entitled to free transport under these Regulations. (6) Subject to the provisions of this regulation, an employee shall be granted transport for the spouse, children or dependants if they accompany or precede or follow him on— (a) first appointment ; (b) transfer; (c) termination of appointment ; (d) convalescent leaves, medical treatment; (e) annual leave; (f) the death of an employee ; or (g) in case of an employee ’s serious illness requiring treatment elsewhere other than his station on the recommendation of the recognized medical practitioner.

provisions.title: Outfit allowance while traveling outside the country
provisions.content: (1) All employees traveling on duty or on training outside country shall be eligible to be paid an unreceipted outfit allowance at a rate approved by the Board from time to time. (2) Payment of outfit allowance under sub-regulation (1) shall be subject to the following conditions— (a) the allowance shall be paid prior to the employee ’s departure from country; (b) the allowance shall be payable once in every period to be determined by the relevant authority and it shall not be paid more than once in respect of one training programme even when the duration of the programme is longer than the determined period; (c) the organization shall pay its lawyers who have been called to the bar a yearly outfit allowance at a rate which shall be determined by the Trustees from time to time.

provisions.title: Other allowances
provisions.content: The Trustees may from time to time and as circumstances permit, introduce allowances other than the allowances specified under these Regulations with a view to facilitating the organization ’s operations and boosting the morale of its employees. NB: After being passed by the Board of Trustees , all allowances will be submitted to the Treasury Registrar for approval.

provisions.title: Transfers
provisions.content: (1) For the purpose of improving efficiency of service, an employee of the organization may be transferred from one station to another where the need arise: Provided that the post of such employee exists at his new station according to the organization ’s Staff Establishment. (2) Where an employee is issued with a letter of transfer, he shall report to his new station within fourteen days. (3) Where spouses who arc both employees of the organization are transferred, only one spouse shall be entitled to transfer dues except for the disturbance allowance which is payable to each of them. (4) Where one of the spouses who are both employees of the organization is transferred he shall be entitled to transfer dues for self, children not exceeding four and his personal effects provided that when the other spouse is transferred to join the former he shall be entitled to disturbance allowance, own fare and personal effects only. (5) There may be temporary transfer whose duration shall not exceed four months: Provided that employees on temporary transfer shall be entitled to subsistence allowance for the entire duration and such employee shall not be permitted to occupy an organization ’s house at the place where he is temporarily transferred. (6) Where an employee is required to undertake duty continuously for more than four consecutive months in one place away from his work station he shall, be regarded as transferred to the place concerned and shall the employee shall be eligible for subsistence and disturbance allowances on the normal way. Part IV – Medical services

provisions.title: Entitlement to medical treatment
provisions.content: (1) Every employee of the organization , their spouses and children shall be— (a) entitled to medical treatment and services at the expense of the organization at designated medical facilities approved by the organization , provided that his illness is not due to his own indiscretion or negligence; (b) subject to the provisions of paragraph (a), every employee , spouse or a child requiring medical treatment shall obtain an approved sick sheet to access befitting medical services. (2) The employees and their families shall be entitled to free dental treatment from either Government or designated hospitals; and such treatment shall include all operative and conservative dental procedures except— (a) partial or full denture; (b) partial or cast metal base; (c) partial or full stainless steel base; (d) repairs and additions to denture; (e) denture relines; (f) orthodontic appliance and dental splints; and (g) gold inlays, crowns and bridhing. (3) The Trustees may, upon advice by relevant medical experts, approve the treatment of an employee , spouse and children outside the country where belter services may be offered and any traveling involved by a medical personnel shall be regarded as traveling on duty. (4) Employees shall be entitled, whilst on duty or training course in an overseas country, to the same benefits as ordinary residents of that country in regard to medical and dental attention under the terms of any national health service that may be available in that country. (5) Where an employee undergoes medical treatment while on duty outside the country he may, in the first instance, pay from his own pocket such expenses as he may have incurred, thereafter, if any claims lies under the terms of this section, he may present such claim for reimbursement suppetrted by the relevant receipts. (6) The Medical Officer may refer an employee , spouse or a child requiring treatment not available in within the country, to any hospital outside the country which provides the required specialist treatment. (7) Employees on salary scales G. 6 to G. 13 shall be entitled to attend treatment at Grade I and those on salary scales G. l to G. 5 and TAN 01 to TAN 05 shall be entitled to treatment at the next lower grade. (8) Subject to the provision of sub-regulation (7) , an employee who opts for a grade higher than his entitlement shall pay the difference in costs. (9) Where medicines or minor medical appliances prescribed by a medical practitioner for the treatment of an employee are certified by a medical officer as being out of stock and cannot be supplied, the employee may purchase such medicines or appliances from a chemist shop and thereafter make a claim supported by relevant receipts and other documents to the Director General for reimbursement. Part V – Advances

provisions.title: Advances in exceptional circumstance
provisions.content: (1) In extreme financial hardships and upon the application in writing, an employee may be granted an advance of up to two months salary, which shall be recovered fully within the same financial year. (2) No employee of the organization may demand an advance as a right and all advances shall be interest free. (3) Under normal circumstances, employees deductions by way of advances and loans shall not exceed one quarter of the gross salary. Part VI – Leave

provisions.title: Leave
provisions.content: (1) The employer shall grant an employee at least twenty eight consecutive days leave in respect of each leave cycle and such leave shall be inclusive of weekends and public holidays that fall within the period of leave. (2) The number of days referred to in sub regulation (1) may be reduced by the number of days which during the leave cycle, at the request of the employee , the employer granted that employee paid occasional leave. (3) The employer may determine when the annual leave is to be taken, provided that it is taken not later than— (a) six months after the end of the leave cycle; or (b) twelve months after the end of the leave cycle, if: (i) the employee has consented; and (ii) the extension is justified by the operational requirements of the employer : Provided that under no circumstances shall leave accumulation go beyond two (2) years cycles. (4) The employer shall pay the employee the remuneration that employee may have been paid had the employee worked during the period of leave before the commencement of the leave. (5) The employer shall not require or permit an employee to take annual leave in place of any leave to which the employee is entitled under this part. (6) The employer shall not require or permit an employee to work for the employer during any period of annual leave unless both parties are in agreement. (7) Subject to the provisions of sub regulation (8), the employer shall not pay an employee an amount of money in substitution for the annual leave to which that employee is entitled, unless both parties are in agreement. (8) he employer shall pay the employee a pro rata amount for annual leave accrued— (a) subject to the provisions of sub regulation (9) , at the termination of employment; or (b) at the expiry of each season in respect of an employee employed on a seasonal basis. (9) An employee is not entitled to be paid any pro rata amount for accrued annual leave if the employee has not taken the leave within the periods and circumstances prescribed in sub regulation (3) (10) The pro rata amount of annual leave referred to in sub regulation (8) shall be calculated at the rate of one day’s basic wage for every thirteen days the employee worked or was entitled to work. (11) An employee on long course is on study leave therefore he shall not be entitled to annual leave. Provided that where an employee attends a course which is less than twelve months, such study leave shall not count in any way against his normal leave entitlement. (12) Every employee shall be entitled to free transport to the place of domicile in the form of cash grant calculated on the basis of current fare rates, once during a two year leave cycle.

provisions.title: Application form for annual leave
provisions.content: Every employee shall fill leave application form TNP/F.7 and submit the same for approval through appropriate channels.

provisions.title: Special leave
provisions.content: (1) The Trustees may, in special circumstances, grant an employee paid special leave to enable him sit for exams approved by the organization and attend meetings or seminars relevant to the organization ’s objectives. (2) The Trustees may grant an employee leave without pay for a period not exceeding two years where such leave is personal but is in the interest of the organization .

provisions.title: Public holidays
provisions.content: The organization shall observe gazetted public holidays as announced by the Government.

provisions.title: Recall from leave to work
provisions.content: An employee who is on leave may be recalled to work to any time before completion of his leave if exigencies of service so require: Provided the where such call to work occurs, the remaining leave days may be granted within the same leave cycle or carried over to the next leave cycle.

provisions.title: Compassionate leave
provisions.content: (1) An employee may be granted paid compassionate leave upon the death of a member of his immediate family , namely spouse, child , parents, mother or father-in-law, grandparent, grandchild and sibling. (2) Compassionate leave shall be separate from the normal annual leave and shall be granted for fourteen (14) days per annum.

provisions.title: Leave to non residents
provisions.content: Employees whose place of domicile is outside Tanzania shall enjoy such privileges as specified in their contracts of employment.

provisions.title: Maternity leave
provisions.content: (1) A female employee shall be entitled to a paid maternity leave as provided in sub regulation (7) of this regulation once in every three years from the date she completed her last maternity leave and such maternity leave shall not be carried forward and shall be exclusive of her annual leave for the calendar year in which the maternity leave was taken. (2) An employee shall give notice to the employer of her intention to take maternity leave at least three months before the expected date of birth and such notice shall be supported by a medical certificate. (3) An employee may commence maternity leave— (a) on the date of delivery; or (b) on an earlier date if a medical practitioner certifies that it is necessary for the employee ’s health or that of her unborn child . (4) No employee shall work within twelve weeks of the birth of her child . (5) Subject to the provisions of sub regulations (2) and (3) , the employee may resume employment on the same terms and conditions of employment at the end of her maternity leave. (6) No employer shall require or permit a pregnant employee or an employee who is nursing a child to perform work that is hazardous to her health or the health of her child . (7) Subject to the provisions of sub regulations (8) and (9) , an employee shall be entitled, within any leave cycle, to at least- (a) eighty four days paid maternity leave; or (b) one hundred days paid maternity leave if such employee gives birth to more than one child at the same time. (8) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub regulation (7) (a) , an employee is entitled to an additional eighty four days paid maternity leave within the leave cycle if the child dies within a year of birth. (9) Subject to the provisions of sub regulation (8) of this regulation the employer is only obliged to grant paid leave for four terms of maternity leave to an employee. (10) Where an employee performs work that is hazardous to her health or that of her child , the employer shall offer her suitable alternative employment, if practicable, on terms and conditions that are no less favourable than her terms and conditions of employment. (11) A female employee shall, within a period not exceeding six months after maternity leave, be allowed to leave the office two hours before the end of office hours every day to breast feed her child . (12) For the purpose of this regulation, “medical certificate” means a certificate issued by a registered medical practitioner, including a midwife, or any other medical practitioner accepted by the employer , which acceptance may not be unreasonably withheld. (13) During any leave cycle a male employee shall be entitled to at least five days paternity leave if— (a) the leave is taken within seven days of the birth of a child ; and (b) the employee is the father of the child . (14) The provisions of this regulation shall apply to all employees appointed on unspecified period of time and those on contractual basis of more than two years: Provided that an employee who does not qualify for maternity leave under this Regulation shall be entitled to six weeks leave after the birth of her child .

provisions.title: Sick leave
provisions.content: (1) Absence from duty on medical grounds is regarded as sick leave and shall be reported by the employee concerned to the Chief Park Warden, Head of Directorate, Head of Department or Director General without delay. (2) The report shall be supported by sick sheet and a certificate from a Medical Officer.

provisions.title: Absence from duty after a spell of illness
provisions.content: (1) Absence from duty may be granted on the recommendation of a Medical Officer for recuperative purposes immediately following a period of sick leave and such absence shall be regarded as convalescent leave. (2) Where a Medical Officer recommends that convalescent leave should be taken at a place in Tanzania other than the employee ’s duty station, then the cost of travel shall be met by the organization and if the period of convalescence recommended exceeds two weeks the organization shall also meet the cost of transporting the employee ’s family .

provisions.title: Medical report
provisions.content: If an employee is repeatedly absent from duty on the ground of ill health and such absence in any one year exceeds twenty-eight working days, then the Director General shall call for a medical examination as per regulation 22 .

provisions.title: Extension of leave on medical grounds
provisions.content: (1) If an employee falls ill so as to require medical attention during his leave and is unable to resume duty on expiry of such leave he shall write to his duty station forwarding at the same time, a certificate from a medical officer stating the nature of his illness and its probable duration. (2) The Management may decide to grant additional leave to the employee to such extent and on such terms as may be recommended by the medical authorities. (3) Unless this order is observed, an employee recommended shall not be entitled to any extension of leave on the grounds of ill-health. (4) No extension of leave on medical grounds shall normally be granted to an employee who has proceeded on leave pending retirement or termination of service.

provisions.title: Limitation of sick and convalescent leave
provisions.content: (1) All employees on permanent terms including those on probation shall normally be permitted local sick or convalescent leave not exceeding six months on full pay and six months on half pay in any “sick period”. (2) Where a need arises for leave in excess of the twelve months the employee shall be on leave without pay. (3) The organization shall arrange for a medical Board to determine the employee ’s retirement on medical grounds as per the provisions of regulation (22) . Part VII – Training

provisions.title: Training policy
provisions.content: (1) As a matter of policy and for the purpose of promoting know how and efficiency, employees training shall be in line with the organization ’s training programme. (2) The organization shall undertake the responsibility of training its employees in line with the organization training needs and programme depending on the availability of funds.

provisions.title: Bonding
provisions.content: (1) An employee who shall be eligible to be considered for long term training shall be confirmed and have served the organization for at least 2 years and have requisite qualifications for admission to the course. (2) An employee selected to attend an in-service course of twelve months or more, on self sponsored or sponsored by the organization , or any donor shall before proceeding on studies enter into agreement with the organization binding himself to serve the organization as shown in appendix TNP/F.9.

provisions.title: Entitlement to full salary
provisions.content: (1) Where an employee attends a full-time course he shall remain subject to the provisions of these Regulations generally and entitled to his gross salary, less any statutory deductions: Provided that an employee who is not in the training programme shall not be entitled to salary. (2) Subject to good conduct and satisfactory progress in his studies, an employee shall be eligible for normal annual increments during his training.

provisions.title: Eligibility for traveling and subsistence support
provisions.content: (1) An employee travelling from his work station to the training institution and vice versa at the start and end of a course as well as on leave or during graduation shall be deemed to be traveling on duty; hence eligible for subsistence allowance on nights necessarily spent.

provisions.title: Failing a course
provisions.content: (1) An employee who fails to proceed with a course as a result of cheating in examinations, indiscipline or breach of the training institution’s Regulations or violation of any written law, disciplinary charges shall be instituted against him basing on the facts which caused discontinuation of the studies;

provisions.title: Successful completion of long course
provisions.content: (1) An employee who successfully completes Postgraduate Degree and who does not receive immediate promotion to a higher grade may be awarded two increments to his salary as may be determined by the Trustees . (2) Where an employee fails an examination he shall not be permitted to sit a second time unless— (a) repetition is permitted by the examining authority; (b) there is a strong recommendation by the institution concerned for the employee to be allowed a second attempt; (c) extenuating circumstances exist such a illness, or failure in the first attempt by a narrow margin; (d) funds are available without adversely affecting the prospects of other employees to be sent for training; and (e) an employee attending a long course shall vacate an organization ’s house. In such circumstances, the employer shall provide transport to his family and personal effects to his place of domicile or any place to be determined by the employee ; (3) An employee intending to pursue further training which is to be sponsored by the employer or employee , including distance learning shall be required to seek permission of the employer before applying for the course. (4) An employee attending distance learning examination shall be given earned leave of seven days for preparation and the examination days as per the respective institution’s time table which shall not be deducted from the annual leave. (5) The organization shall permit staff to pursue distance learning as per the Training Policy and relevant Training Programme. (6) An employee who sits for supplementary examinations or repeating a year shall be entitled to upkeep expenses of such amount payable according to the relevant year’s fee structure as approved by the relevant institution, subject to the following conditions— (a) repetition is permitted by the examining institution; (b) there is a strong recommendation by the institution concerned for the employee to be allowed a second attempt; (c) extenuating circumstances exist such as illness or failure in the first attempt by a narrow margin; and (d) funds are available without adversely affecting the prospects of other employees to be sent for training. (7) There shall be a training committee which will be responsible for all matters relating to staff training such as training programme and budget to cover both internal and external courses, seminars, workshops and study tours. The committee shall also be responsible for proposing review of the training policy whenever necessary. (8) The training committee shall comprise of the Director General, Directors and Human Resources Manager for the Headquarters and Park Manager, Human Resources and Administrative Officer, Park Accountant and Park Warden Protection for the Parks. The Committee may co opt any other person as may be deemed necessary. The Park Training Committee shall forward the training proposals to the Headquarters Training Committee for further consideration and approval.

provisions.title: Study leave
provisions.content: (1) An employee selected to attend an in-service course shall be granted— (a) in the case of higher education, which is in the organization training programme, leave with pay; (b) in the case of higher education which is not in the organization training programme, leave without pay provided that he has completed two years in the service; (c) in the case of other courses, other than of higher education an employee shall be given a special leave of absence with pay. (2) An employee aspiring to sit for examinations conducted by certain professional boards such as National Board for Accountants and Auditors, Board for Materials Management, is normally required to attend review courses. In order to assist an employee to comply with such professional bodies’ requirements and at the same time minimize costs on the part of the organization , the following special arrangements shall apply— (a) the review courses are conducted by approved and recognized by training institutions; (b) an employee shall be granted special leave of absence with pay to enable him to attend and complete the course; (c) where an employee is attending the course away from his duty station he shall be paid transport and travelling privileges and subsistence allowance; (d) an employee who has attended a long course and fail the final examination both initially and after the grace period offered by any such professional body may be permitted to attend a review course at his own expense; (e) in all other cases an employee may be permitted to attend a review course and sit for an examination at the organization ’s expenses once for each part of the examination; and (f) where an employee attends a short course which has conditions to assess transfer of knowledge including specific exercises and tests, an employee shall ensure that he attends the course in full and fulfills all the conditions specified. The organization shall make follow-ups and an employee shall be required to produce evidence of attendance and completion of the course. Part VIII – Accommodation

provisions.title: Housing committees
provisions.content: (1) The organization shall endeavour to provide accommodation to all its employees. (2) The organization shall form housing allocation committees comprising of officers appointed by the Director General or Park Managers as the case may be, for the purpose of allocating accommodation to employees. (3) Employees shall reside in the allocated accommodation free of charge provided that the employee shall pay for utility bills.

provisions.title: No letting or sub-letting of quarters
provisions.content: (1) Under no circumstances shall an employee let or sub-let the organization ’s residential house allocated to him or any part of such house or its grounds and it applies in all circumstances including those where an employee has been permitted to retain the organization ’s house during his absence from duty on leave or other cause. (2) An employee who is allocated a residential house is responsible for maintaining it in a clean and satisfactory condition, of the premises, grounds, fittings and fixtures, and shall be liable to all damages and dilapidations which are not due to fair wear and tear. (3) Where it becomes necessary to redecorate or recondition the quarters or the premises to their original condition solely as a result of the negligence or carelessness of an employee , the employee shall be liable to be surcharged with the cost involved. (4) Applications for any repairs or alterations to the staff quarters or to the wiring or fittings must be made to officers authorized by the Director General for that purpose. (5) Employees are not permitted to extend, remove or make any alterations to the staff quarters. (6) An employee vacating an organization ’s house should hand over the keys of the same to the Director General, or Park Manager. (7) Where furniture is provided for, the occupant of an organization ’s house shall sign an inventory detailing all the furniture in his custody which inventory shall be kept by the Director General or Park Manager for that purpose. (8) The occupant shall become personally responsible for the furniture, including the liability to make good any unreasonable loss or damage.

provisions.title: Handing over furniture to new occupant
provisions.content: On vacating staff quarters, furniture should be handed over by the outgoing employee to the incoming occupant in the presence of the Administrative Manager for Headquarters and Human Resources and Administrative Officer for the Parks. Part IX – Uniforms

provisions.title: Uniforms for employees
provisions.content: (1) The Trustees shall supply uniforms (including designated paramilitary and ceremonial dress), clothing and relevant equipment to the employees in accordance with Board ’s approval to various categories of staff. (2) Uniforms shall be made in a special standard design and pattern and as per each category of staff as shall be determined by Trustees . (3) Every employee shall wear the organization uniform while on duty and shall keep it clean and in good order. (4) The organization uniforms shall uniforms not be worn by employees while off duty. (5) Uniforms of the organization shall not be sold or disposed of in any manner except with the prior authority of the Director General, or Park Manager as the case may be. (6) An employee who is issued with paramilitary attire is required to return them to the employer upon cessation of employment. Part X – Disciplinary process

provisions.title: General
provisions.content: (1) All employees, shall abide by these staff Regulations and shall in addition abide by specific instructions issued to them by the Trustees or Director General either directly or through senior officers. (2) All employees shall conduct themselves in a manner designed to safe-guard the interest, good name and well being of the organization , any employee failing to conduct himself in such manner through willful acts, omission or negligence shall be guilty of misconduct subject to disciplinary action. (3) An employee shall be responsible for safe-guarding information in respect the affairs of the Trustees which he may acquire in the course of his duties, of which its disclosure is likely to be prejudicial to the interests of the organization . (4) Improper disclosure of such information by unauthorized employee shall constitute a gross misconduct.

provisions.title: Board of trustees
provisions.content: Disciplinary authority over all employees shall be the Trustees but for practical implementation the Trustees have delegated some of such authority to the management in the following manner— (a) in respect of holders of posts whose salary scale is G. 7 and above, the appointing and disciplinary authority shall be the Board of Trustees ; (b) the Management’s Appointments and Disciplinary Committee is delegated power by the Board of Trustees to act on its behalf as the appointing and disciplinary authority in respect of all employees holding posts whose salary scale is G. 1 to G. 6 and TAN 01 to TAN 05.

provisions.title: Interdiction
provisions.content: (1) Where the disciplinary authority considers that it is in the organization interest that an employee should cease forthwith to perform the duties and functions of his office, the disciplinary authority or any delegated disciplinary authority as the case may be, may interdict the employee from performing those duties and functions, provided that the charges have been served against that employee . (2) Where the employee is interdicted under this Regulation, such employee shall be informed in writing of the reasons for such interdiction. (3) Where the charges served against an employee are defective, the disciplinary authority shall be allowed to substitute them with the proper charges not later than fourteen days from the date when the former charges were served. (4) An employee under interdiction may not leave his duty station without the prior permission in writing, of the disciplinary authority . (5) Where an employee has been convicted of a criminal offence the disciplinary authority shall suspend that employee from performing his duties and functions pending consideration of the case on disciplinary grounds under the provisions of these Regulations. (6) An employee on suspension or interdiction shall be entitled to full pay but shall not be entitled to any allowances except for medical treatment, housing and utilities where applicable.

provisions.title: Employee charged of an offence
provisions.content: (1) Whenever an employee is charged of an offence which calls for a penalty other than a reprimand, the appropriate disciplinary authority shall cause investigation to be made on the alleged offence and shall call for a full report on the investigation in writing. (2) The appropriate disciplinary authority shall then afford the employee a right to be heard by written reply and thereafter hearing further oral defence or require him to submit additional defence in writing if he so chooses. (3) Where an employee chooses to submit further defence in writing to the disciplinary authority than appearing before it and make oral defence over and above his written reply to the disciplinary charge that has been served upon him, he shall be deemed to have been afforded fully his right to be heard. (4) No member of disciplinary authority who is involved in investigation of the offence stated in sub-regulation (3) of this regulation shall sit in the committee during deliberations leading to final decision provided that such member shall be entitled to present his report to the appropriate committee and then vacate the room to enable other members deliberate and make final decision on the alleged offence.

provisions.title: Formal proceedings
provisions.content: For purposes of these part, “formal proceedings” means proceedings likely to result in dismissal, termination, reduction in rank, termination of appointment or reduction in salary of the employee should he be found guilty of the offence charged against him.

provisions.title: Procedure in formal proceedings
provisions.content: (1) No formal proceedings for disciplinary offence shall be instituted against an employee unless he has been served with a copy of the charge setting out the nature of the offence which he is alleged to have committed and a disciplinary charge or charges may be in the form laid down in Part A of the Second Schedule to these Regulations. (2) Every disciplinary charge shall state briefly the nature of the offence which the accused is alleged to have committed and shall set out in a concise form the allegations made against the accused employee . (3) After the charge has been drawn-up it shall be served upon the employee . (4) Every charge shall be accompanied by a letter or notice addressed to the accused employee requiring him to state in writing, within such reasonable period of not more than ten days, as may be specified in the notice, the grounds upon which he relies to exculpate himself and a notice may be in the form laid down in Part B of the second schedule to these Regulations. (5) The employer shall notify the employee of the allegations facing him, a hearing date, place and time using a form and language that the employee can reasonably understand. (6) The employer shall further notify the employee of his right to be represented by a trade union representative or a fellow employee of his choice who has no conflict of interest, both of whom should be from within the organization . (7) The employer shall bear the cost for the employee and his representative on attending the hearing before his Disciplinary Authority. (8) No formal proceedings instituted under these Regulations shall be invalid by reason only of an irregularity in the formulation of the charge. (9) Where an employee has been served with a charge and fails to make representations in writing giving ground upon which he relies to exculpate himself within the period specified in the notice accompanying the charge or make representations which in the opinion of the Disciplinary Authority do not amount to a complete defence of the offence of which the accused employee is charged, the Disciplinary Authority may require an accused employee to make oral defence and further clarification before reaching any decision.

provisions.title: Investigation officer
provisions.content: (1) Where it is necessary to institute disciplinary proceedings against an employee , the Disciplinary Authority shall make preliminary investigations before instituting the disciplinary proceedings. (2) No person shall be appointed an investigation officer unless he is in a salary scale of a rank higher than the rank held by the accused employee . (3) There shall be appointed an ad hoc investigation committee where disciplinary proceeding is envisaged. (4) Where the officer conducting the investigation is of the opinion that it will be desirable for him to be assisted in the conduct of the investigation by persons who may be more conversant with any professional or technical matter likely to arise in the course of the proceedings, he shall request the assignment of not more than two public officers or organization ’s staff with the necessary qualifications and experience to assist him. (5) The report of the investigation officer shall not contain recommendations as to the form or nature of the punishment or disciplinary action to be taken.

provisions.title: Summary proceedings
provisions.content: Summary proceedings shall be instituted where the alleged offence is not of such gravity as to warrant a dismissal, reduction in rank or reduction in salary.

provisions.title: Procedure in summary proceedings
provisions.content: (1) Summary proceedings shall be instituted by a letter addressed to the employee setting out the allegations against the employee and informing the employee of the penalty intended to be imposed and requiring the employee to show cause in writing within a specified period why the penalty should not be imposed. (2) On receipt of the response of the employee or if there is no response within the specified period of time, the Disciplinary Authority may impose the penalty of a fine or stoppage of increment in payment depending on the gravity of the disciplinary breach.

provisions.title: Procedure in disciplinary offence amounting to criminal offence
provisions.content: (1) Where an employee is alleged to have committed a disciplinary offence which also constitutes a criminal offence under any written law, and proceedings for the criminal offence (hereinafter referred to as “criminal proceedings”) are instituted against him in a court of law— (a) the appropriate Disciplinary authority may suspend the employee from his duties on full pay pending finalization of the case; (b) the disciplinary authority shall, upon the suspended employee ’s acquittal, reinstate the employee .

provisions.title: Convicted employee deemed guilty
provisions.content: (1) Where an employee has been convicted of a criminal offence, he shall be deemed to have been guilty under these Regulations and for purposes of these Regulations criminal under these Regulations proceedings shall be deemed to have been concluded— (a) where there has been no appeal against conviction or acquittal and upon the expiry of the time allowed for such appeal by or under any written law; (b) where there has been an appeal or further appeal against any conviction or acquittal and the appellate court has given its decision.

provisions.title: Imposition of disciplinary penalty not bar to criminal prosecution
provisions.content: Nothing in these Regulations and no imposition of a disciplinary penalty for a breach of these Regulations shall exempt any employee from criminal prosecution.

provisions.title: Appeals
provisions.content: (1) In every disciplinary proceedings under these Regulations the accused employee shall be entitled to be informed of findings and reasons for the decision in writing. (2) An employee who is aggrieved by the decision of the Disciplinary authority in formal disciplinary proceedings may appeal to the appellate authority in writing copied to the disciplinary authority . (3) The appeal shall be made within thirty (30) days of the decision of the disciplinary authority provided that where statutory period fixed by labour laws is at variance with the period set in this Regulation such statutory period shall prevail. (4) Appellate authority shall be those statutory bodies created by the labour laws in force. (5) The procedure on appeal shall be that prescribed by the relevant labour laws in force.

provisions.title: Disciplinary offences
provisions.content: (1) Where an employee is absent from duty without leave or reasonable cause for a period exceeding five (5) consecutive days shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (2) Where an employee , impairs his efficiency in performance of his work by becoming intoxicated during working hours shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (3) Where an employee omits to obey any proper command or instruction of any employee placed in authority over him shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (4) Where an employee commits any unjustifiable assault or is involved in brawls at the place in the course of work or uses abusive or insulting language or is guilty of insulting behaviour to any client, shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (5) Where an employee commits an unjustifiable assault, at his place of work or outside his place of work on members of the management , his work-mates, visitors or clients, shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (6) Where an employee misuses or misappropriates funds, resources or willfully damages, misuses or misappropriates buildings, machinery, aircraft, ship, boat, canoe, vehicle, raw materials, other property or tools or any object used in connection with his work, shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (7) Where an employee neglects or fails to carry out his duties so as to endanger himself or others or property or neglects or fails to comply with any instructions relating to safety or welfare commits a gross misconduct. (8) Where an employee commits any serious or repeated act of insubordination at the employer ’s premises or during working hours against the employer , or members of the management staff shall in his behaviour be commits a gross misconduct. (9) Where an employee is unable to perform his work efficiently by reason of improper use of drugs, commits a gross misconduct. (10) Where an employee is convicted by any Court of Law for any unlawful act at the place of or in the course of work, unless such employee successfully appeals against such conviction commits a gross misconduct. (11) Where an employee is guilty of an immoral act at the place of or in the course of work, shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (12) Where an employee smokes in a place which the employer has forbidden for reasons of safety, shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (13) Where an employee is convicted by the Court of Law of an offence involving fraud or dishonesty, or for which he was sentenced to imprisonment, unless such employee successfully appeals against such conviction, commits a gross misconduct. (14) Where an employee without due authority discloses or conveys any information or any technical, trade or confidential matter to the prejudice of his employer , commits a gross misconduct. (15) Where an employee is involved in any act or omission involving or resulting into immoral turpitude such as theft or corruption, shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (16) Where an employee being employed in the service of the organization commits any act which is against organization interest, commits a gross misconduct. (17) Where an employee is discovered to have given false information to enable him secure employment or promotion in the organization , shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (18) An employee shall be responsible for safeguarding information in respect of affairs of the Trustees which he may acquire in the course of his duties of which its disclosure is likely to be prejudicial to the interests of the organization . Improper disclosure of such information by unauthorized employee shall render him guilty of gross misconduct. (19) Where an employee willfully gives any unauthorized person arms or ammunition shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (20) Where an employee is guilty of cowardice shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (21) Where an employee leaves his post or place of duty before he is properly relieved except if he is in hot pursuit of an offender he is obliged to apprehend shall be guilt of gross misconduct.
