(22) Where an employee is found asleep while on duty shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (23) Where an employee behaves, speaks or uses any gesture or other means showing or calculated to show disrespect to any senior officer shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (24) Where an employee malingers or feigns any disease or infirmity or willfully causes to himself any disease or infirmity shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (25) Where an employee drinks intoxicating liquor while on duty shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (26) Where an employee discharges or causes to be discharged any Firearms or ammunition without orders from Senior Officer and without reasonable or lawful cause shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (27) Where an employee sells, pawns, loses by neglect, makes away with, destroys or willfully or negligently damages, or fails to report any loss or damage to any arms, ammunition, accoutrement, uniform or other article of personal use or any vehicle or other property committed to his charge belonging to the organization , donors or for which the Government of Tanzania is responsible shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (28) Where an employee fails to execute his duties in protection of wildlife shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (29) Where an employee without lawful authority kills, destroys or wounds any wildlife or habitat or in any manner makes away-with or disposes of the same shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (30) Where an employee either willfully or by negligence, does an act or makes an omission in flying an aircraft or piloting a sea-going boat, canoe or vessel of the organization or in the use of that aircraft or boat, canoe, vessel or in relation to that aircraft or boat, canoe, vessel or to aircraft or vessel material, which causes or is likely to cause loss of life or bodily injury to any person or unnecessary annoyance to any person or animal shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (31) Where an employee either willfully or by negligence allows the escape of any suspect or accused person placed under his custody or without lawful authority or justification releases such person from his custody shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (32) Where an employee neglects or refuses to assist in the apprehension of any member of the organization or of any other person charged with any offence, when lawfully ordered so to do shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (33) Where an employee is avoiding or resisting lawful arrest or escaping from lawful custody shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (34) Where an employee makes statement affecting the character of another member of the organization or of any other person which he knows to be false or does not believe to be true, or willfully expresses any material facts, or makes, or participates in making, any anonymous complaint shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (35) Where an employee is found in improper possession of game trophy, game meat, timber products or habitat shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (36) Where an employee contravenes, refuses or fails to comply with any provision of the Law, Regulations, Standing Orders, or other routine Orders of the organization which have the effect of causing irreparable breakdown of the employment relationship shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (37) Where an employee refuses to be tested for alcohol or illicit drugs or evidently found drunk while on duty shall be guilty of gross misconduct. (38) Where an employee refuses to comply with a transfer order shall be guilty of gross misconduct.

provisions.title: Punishment for misconduct offences
provisions.content: Employees who commit disciplinary offences under regulation 88 of these Regulations which constitute gross misconduct shall be terminated, provided that the Disciplinary Authority may impose a lesser penalty when deemed necessary.

provisions.title: Other punishments
provisions.content: Employees who commit disciplinary offences set out in this regulation which constitute misconduct shall be served with written warning, final written warning, comprehensive final warning followed by termination: Provided that where it so requires, provisions of the country’s labor law will be followed— (a) where an employee fails to complete his pertinent task; (b) where an employee neglects his duties but not so as to endanger the safety of persons or property; (c) where an employee is habitually absent from his work place during working hours without his employer ’s permission; (d) where an employee is habitually absent from his work place without reasonable cause; (e) where an employee is habitually reporting late for work; (f) where an employee fails to comply with the employer ’s instructions relating to work including, without prejudice to he generality of the foregoing, those designed to increase efficiency or output; (g) where an employee without reasonable cause, fails to attend or arrives late at a required point of duty of leaves before he is permitted or properly relieved is guilty of misconduct; and (h) where an employee appears on duty untidy or dirty in his person, arms, clothing or equipment shall be guilty of misconduct. (i) where an employee contravenes any of the organization rules, orders or directives including code of conduct shall be guilty of misconduct; (j) where an employee is idle in the performance of his duty shall be guilty of misconduct; (k) where an employee receives an adverse performance appraisal report shall be guilty of misconduct.

provisions.title: Limitation of time for misconduct offences
provisions.content: If an employee is charged with disciplinary offences for misconduct set out in Regulation 88 , no previous breach shall be taken into account if the employee has not committed a breach of the same offence within a period of six months immediately preceding the breach under consideration. Part XI – Workers’ Council and Trade Union

provisions.title: Workers Council
provisions.content: (1) There shall be established a Workers’ Council which shall exercise such functions in accordance with the principles and practices defined and remaining in accordance to the Public Service (Negotiating Machinery) Act [Cap. 105] (2) The Workers’ Council’s activities shall be conducted in such a manner that docs not interfere with the day-to-day work and conduct of business of the organization . (3) The provisions of the Public Service (negotiating Machinery) Act , shall have effect as to Master Workers Council composition, tenure of office, proceedings of meetings and other matters relating to the Council. [Cap. 105]

provisions.title: Functions of the Master Workers Council
provisions.content: (1) The functions of the Master Workers Council shall include— (a) to advise the organization on procedures for promotion and disciplinary measures in respect of organization employees; (b) to advise the organization on measures to be taken in order to ensure effective delivery of services; (c) to advise the organization on the changes that are to be made to the organization ’s staff regulations, conditions of work and service; (2) Where the Master Workers Council decides to make recommendations to the organization , it shall submit such recommendations through the Board of Trustees . (3) The Master Workers Council shall cause in every year to be prepared and published a report and submit it to the Board of Trustees . (4) Upon receipt of the report, the Board of Trustees may consider such recommendations and adopt them for purposes of improving the organization service.

provisions.title: Employees other than Paramilitary employees may be members of Trade Union
provisions.content: (1) Every employee of the organization except for Paramilitary employees may be members of Trade Union in consonance with labour laws subject to the establishment of the paramilitary employees’ code of ethics. (2) For purpose of clarity, membership of Paramilitary employees to trade unions, effected prior to the making and coming into operation of the Revised National Parks Act , shall thereby be dissolved. (3) The employer shall deduct dues of a register Trade Union from an employee ’s wages if that employee has authorized the employer to do so in the prescribed form and remit such deductions to the Trade Union within seven days after the end of the month in which the deductions are made. (4) An employee of the organization shall not be compelled to become a member of a trade union without his express free will. Part XII – General and miscellaneous provisions

provisions.title: The Code of Ethics and Conduct
provisions.content: There shall be a Code of Ethics and Conduct for the organization in the form shown in the First Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Employees awareness of the Code
provisions.content: (1) Any person on first appointment to the organization he shall be provided with a copy of the Code of Ethics and Conduct of the organization . (2) Every employee shall be required to sign a declaration of compliance with the code.

provisions.title: Attendance register
provisions.content: (1) Supervising officers shall ensure that employees report for duty in time and do not leave their offices before the official closing time. (2) In order to maintain a record of the time of arrival of employees on duty, attendance registers shall be maintained by organization in the form set out in Appendix TNP/F.... The register shall be signed by members of the staff at all levels. (3) The supervising officer in the office shall be responsible for the proper maintenance of the register which shall usually be kept at a convenient place in the office premises and shall ensure that the register is available for signature as follows— (a) before the commencement of every working session; a blue or black line shall be drawn at 8:00 a.m. immediately below the last signature of employees who have signed the register, and Column 5 of the register shall be signed; (b) after the commencement of the working session, a red line shall be drawn at 8:15 a. m. immediately below the last signature of the employees who have signed the register and column 5 of the register in his presence; (c) the register shall be withdrawn at 8:30 a.m. and employees who report for duty after the attendance register has been withdrawn shall report to the supervising officer and sign the register in his presence; (d) approved late attendance shall be recorded by the supervising officer in the remarks column; (e) the supervising officer shall submit the attendance register to the Head of Directorate for Inspection on the 15 th and 30 th day of every month; (f) prompt disciplinary action shall be taken against public servants who report late for duty and those who fail to observe the provisions of this Regulation; and (g) employees who constantly report for duty on time should be commended.

provisions.title: Effect of Breach of the Code
provisions.content: Breach of the Code by an employee shall have serious consequences including disciplinary action or criminal prosecution.

provisions.title: Policy on HIV/AIDS
provisions.content: There shall be a workplace policy on HIV/AIDS as approved by the Board of Trustees .

provisions.title: Revocation of GN No. 374 of 2005
provisions.content: The Tanzania National Parks Staff Regulations are hereby revoked. First Schedule (Made under regulation 96) Code of ethics and conduct for employees I. Ethical conduct and behaviour In order for the employee to be efficient and respected, employees behave and conduct themselves in a manner as stipulated below: 1. Respect all Human Rights and be courteous. 2. Perform their duty diligently and in disciplined manner. 3. Promote teamwork. 4. Pursue Excellency in the service. 5. Exercise responsibility and good stewardship. 6. Promote transparency and accountability. 7. Discharge duties with integrity; and 8. Maintain political neutrality This code will explain each of these expectations in more detail. II. Respect human rights and being courteous 1. Democratic rights religions An employee has the right of being a member of any Political Party and vote both for his/her Political party and in general elections. 2. An employee can become a member of any religious sect provided that he/she does not contravene the existing laws. However, since the government has no religion, religious beliefs should not be advocated in Tanzania National Parks offices. 3. Courtesy to all An employee shall not discriminate or harass a member of the public or a junior employee on grounds of sex, tribe, religion, nationality, ethnicity, marital status, H1V/AIDS, pregnancy, political affiliation, age or disability. 4. An employee shall be courteous to senior and fellow employees as well as all clients and particularly the clients being served. If an employee is requested to clarify or to provide direction or issue arising from laws, regulations, procedures, the employee will do so with clarity and promptness. 5. Respect for all An employee will respect other employees, their rights, as well as their right to privacy especially when handling private and personal information. 6. Sexual harassment An employee shall refrain from having sexual relationships at the work place likewise he/she will avoid all types of conduct which may constitute sexual harassment which include— (a) Pressure for sexual activity or sexual favours with a fellow employee; (b) Rape, sexual battery and molestation or any sexual assault; (c) Intentional physical conduct which is sexual in nature such as unwelcome touching, pinching, panting, grabbing and or brushing against another employee’s body, hair or clothes; (d) Sexual innuendoes, gestures, noises, jokes, comments or remarks to another person about one’s sexuality or body; (e) Offering or receiving preferential treatment, promises or rewards and offering or submitting to sexual favours. III – Discipline and diligence 1. Discrimination discipline For efficient performance an employee will perform his/her duties diligently with a high degree of discipline. An employee shall therefore use the time, skill and expertise one has so as to attain the expected goals. Employees are expected to— (a) obey the law; decisions should not be biased because of religion, ethnicity, sex, personal interests or any relationship; (b) obey and effect lawful directives; (c) carry out assigned duties efficiently and where an employee considers that he/she is being asked to act improperly he/she shall report the matter to his/her superiors in accordance with the laid down procedures; (d) be ready to work at any duty station; (e) refrain from any conduct which might impair one’s work performance; (f) keep punctuality with respect to hours of arrival at work, at all official appointments or engagements and not absent oneself from duty without proper authorization or reasonable cause; (g) avoid the use of rude and abusive language; (h) finish assigned duties within required time and standards; 2. Attire An employee will maintain personal hygiene, dress in respectable attire in accordance with the acceptable norms of the office as stipulated in staff circulars. 3. Private life While out of office, an employee will conduct his/her personal life in such a manner that it does not affect his/her services or bring the service into disrepute. He/she is therefore required to refrain from becoming drunk, using narcotic drugs and any other unacceptable behaviour. 4. Secret and confidentiality An employee shall not disclose confidential or official information which has been communicated to or has been availed while discharging official duties without due permission. An employee shall continue to maintain secrecy and confidentiality of official information even after one has left the Service— (a) an employee shall not use any official document or photocopy such as letter or any other document or information obtained in the course of discharging his/her duties for personal ends; (b) an employee shall not communicate with the media on issues related to work or official policy without due permission; and (c) official information will be released to the media by officials who have been authorized to do so according to the laid down procedures. IV – Team work 1. Team work Employees will strive to promote teamwork by offering help to co-employees whenever the need so arises. Team work will be achieved by employees observing the following— (a) giving instructions which are clear and undistorted; (b) giving due weight and consideration to official views submitted by fellow employees and subordinates; (c) ensuring that subordinates clearly understand the scope of their work and encourage them to enhance their competence and skills; (d) giving credit to an employee with outstanding performance and not seeking personal credit at the employee’s expense; (e) avoid malicious actions or words intended to ridicule either subordinates or superiors; (f) reporting on his/her subordinates to be done fairly and without any fear or favour. V – Pursuing of Excellence in service 1. Excellence in service Since the service is geared toward provision of excellent services, employees will do the following— (a) strive to achieve the highest standards of performance; (b) if a member of professional body then adhere to their respective professional Code of Conduct; (c) strive to acquire new knowledge and skills continuously and use them effectively; (d) recognize the need for training and strive to get such training. VI – Exercise responsibility and good stewardship 1. Exercising responsibilities An employee shall act within the boundaries of the authority and the responsibility delegated. In doing so, he/she shall— (a) make decisions in line with authorized standards and procedures; and (b) discharge duties effectively and be accountable for one’s own action. 2. Organization property An employee shall safeguard organization’s funds and other properties of the organization entrusted to him/her and shall ensure that no damage, loss, misappropriation occurs to the funds or organization property. VII – Transparency and accountability 1. An employee will adhere to and practice meritocratic principles in appointment, promotions and while delivering any service, he/she will be accountable both for actions and inactions through normal tiers of authority. 2. (1) An employee shall conduct meetings for the purpose of promoting efficiency and shall not use meeting as a way of avoiding being responsible for the decision he is supposed to have made on his own. (2) An employee shall not engage in unofficial activities or projects during official hours or conduct such activities or projects within the office premises or by using public property. (3) An employee shall be ready to declare his/her property or that of his/her spouse when required to do so. (3) Loyalty to the Government An employee shall be Loyal to the duly constituted Government of the day and will therefore implement policies and decisions given by the Minister or any other Government leader. [Please note: Numbering as in original.] VIII – Discharge duties with integrity 1. Adherence to laws corruption An employee shall not fear to abide to Laws, Regulations and Procedures when discharging his/her duties. 2. An employee shall not solicit, force or accept bribes from a person whom he/she is serving, has already served or will be serving either by doing so in person or by using another person. 3 Pecuniary Embarrassment (1) An employee or any member of his/her family shall not receive Presents in form of money, entertainments or any service from a person that may be regarded as geared towards compromising his/her integrity. (2) An employee may accept or give nominal gifts such as pens, calendar and diaries in small amount. (3) An employee will return to the donor any other gift or hand them over to the organization, in which case a receipt will be issued. (4) An employee shall perform his/her duties honestly and impartially to avoid circumstances that may lead to conflict of interest. If conflict of interest arises he/she shall inform his/her superiors who will decide upon the best course of action to resolve it. 4. Conflict of interest An employee shall not borrow to the extent of not being able to repay the debts as this will discredit the organization as well as affect his/her ability to make unbiased decisions. IX – Political neutrality 1. Political parties An employee can participate in politics provided that when so doing he/she observes the following— (a) shall not conduct or engage oneself with political activities during official hours or at work premises; (b) shall not take part in political activity, which will compromise or be seen to compromise his/her loyalty to the Government; (c) shall not provide services with bias due to his/her political affiliation. Although employees have a right to communicate with their political representatives they— (a) shall not use such influence to intervene on matters affecting him/her, which are in dispute between the government or organization and him; (b) shall not use such influence for furthering personal ends, which are not part of the government organization policy. X – Conclusion 1. The Code of Ethics and Conduct has been issued pursuant to the National Parks Act Cap. 282 of 2002 Revised Edition as amended under section 13A of GN. No. l 1 of 2003 and a breach of the Code will be dealt with under the Tanzania National Parks Staff Regulations or other relevant law. 2. For efficient, effective and courteous delivery of services to the public the organization employee must observe this Code.
